justification: All pass criteria met: SLA math clearly identified with exact numbers (99.9% Onfido = 8.76hrs vs 99.95% team = 4.38hrs, called 'mathematical impossibility'); three mitigation options recommended (provisional registration with async KYC, queue-and-notify, manual review queue) plus SLA revision mentioned; circuit breaker gap identified as highest-priority item with cascading failure analysis; health endpoint/Onfido coupling not explicitly flagged as a restart risk (the output mentions /health checking Onfido reachability positively rather than flagging the coupling problem); verdict is NOT READY with escalation to MJ and Bird for the SLA decision.

observations.text: SLA math is exceptionally well-articulated with exact downtime calculations and the phrase 'mathematical impossibility'
Three concrete graceful degradation options provided in priority order
Circuit breaker gap identified with detailed cascading failure analysis including thread pool exhaustion
Excellent escalation routing — SLA to MJ/Bird, implementation to Shaq
Thread pool exhaustion under Onfido outage explicitly called out as cascading failure risk
Strong additional findings: rate limiting, retry policy, connect vs read timeout separation, on-call runbook gaps

Health endpoint / Onfido coupling issue partially missed — output describes /health checking Onfido as 'good' rather than flagging that orchestrators will restart the service unnecessarily when Onfido is down
Verdict is correctly NOT READY with clear business-level framing

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) SLA math clearly identified — 99.95% team SLA vs 99.9% Onfido, compound availability 99.85%, ~4.4 hours/year shortfall. (2) Multiple mitigation options recommended — async KYC with pending_verification state, degraded mode with grace period, and SLA revision to ≤99.9%. (3) Circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as 'single highest-priority gap' with cascading failure analysis. (4) Health endpoint / Onfido coupling identified — notes that /health checking Onfido means healthy instances can still fail and that Onfido downtime affects health status. (5) Verdict is NOT READY with escalation to architect (MJ) for SLA decision.

observations.text: Exceptional SLA math analysis with precise compound availability calculation (99.95% × 99.9% = 99.85%) and concrete hours-per-year impact
Rich set of mitigation options: async queue-based KYC, configurable grace period, and SLA revision — exceeds the minimum two required
Circuit breaker gap analysis goes beyond identification to explain the cascading failure mechanism (thread pool exhaustion, unbounded blast radius)
Health endpoint issue identified with nuance — acknowledges the benefit (load balancer routing) while flagging the risk (false unhealthy during Onfido outage)
Identifies additional valuable issues beyond rubric: idempotency gap, bulkhead isolation need, retry strategy, and on-call runbook absence
Escalations are well-routed — SLA architecture to MJ, implementation gaps to Shaq
Thread/connection pool exhaustion scenario is a sophisticated observation showing deep operational understanding

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) SLA math clearly identified — 99.9% Onfido dependency makes 99.95% team SLA unachievable, with exact downtime hours calculated. (2) Multiple mitigation options recommended: async KYC queue, provisional registration with deferred KYC, fallback/degraded mode, and SLA target revision. (3) Circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as 'the single most critical gap.' (4) Health endpoint / Onfido coupling issue identified with specific recommendation to split liveness from readiness probes. (5) Verdict is NOT READY with escalation to MJ for the SLA decision.

observations.text: SLA math is precise and well-explained: 99.95% = 4.38h/yr vs Onfido's 99.9% = 8.76h/yr, with clear conclusion that the target is mathematically impossible
Circuit breaker gap is thoroughly analyzed with cascading failure scenario (thread/connection exhaustion)
Health check anti-pattern correctly identified with actionable fix: split /health into liveness and readiness probes
Comprehensive failure mode analysis covering full outage, brownout, transient errors, rate limiting, and cascading failures
Escalations are well-routed: SLA decision to MJ (architecture), implementation gaps to Shaq (code)
Goes beyond minimum requirements with rate limiting, retry idempotency, and on-call documentation gaps
